Land Rover Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SDV6 Hybrid engine (306DT with hybrid system) typically costs £4,500–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,300–£2,200. As a low‑volume hybrid variant, supply is limited – prices are indicative. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The SDV6 Hybrid shares the 306DT diesel engine with the standard SDV6, inheriting the same crankshaft‑bearing vulnerability. The hybrid system adds complexity – transmission clutch plate wear is a known issue, and the lithium‑ion battery may degrade with age. With strict maintenance and regular use, it can be a reliable and efficient powertrain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most serious issues are crankshaft bearing failure (shared with standard SDV6), hybrid transmission clutch plate wear (NHTSA recall), hybrid battery degradation, and DPF blockage from urban driving. The vehicle is designed for long‑distance motorway driving.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the Range Rover Sport's retained value (typically £15,000–£35,000+), a £4,500–£7,500 reconditioned engine is a significant but often justifiable expense. However, if the hybrid battery has also failed (costing £2,000–£5,000) and parts may be unavailable, the total repair cost may approach or exceed the vehicle's value. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The SDV6 Hybrid uses the 306DT engine code combined with a 35 kW electric motor. This is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The SDV6 Hybrid badge and the presence of the hybrid system (battery under the floor, EV mode button) confirm the variant.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an SDV6 Hybrid issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – DPF blockage, turbocharger wear, oil leaks – are repairable. However, crankshaft bearing failure usually requires full engine replacement. Hybrid battery degradation may require specialist refurbishment as dealer replacement may no longer be available. Transmission clutch plate wear may be covered by recall – check your VIN first.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the Range Rover Sport L494 SDV6 Hybrid. As a low‑volume hybrid variant, supply of used and reconditioned units is limited — prices are indicative. The hybrid system components (battery, electric motor, inverter) add complexity and cost to any replacement.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid engine typically takes 14–20 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent Land Rover specialist). Hybrid system integration adds to the complexity.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Crankshaft Bearing Failure (Shared with 306DT Diesel)
problems.symptoms: Sudden loud knocking from the engine, oil pressure warning light, complete engine seizure — often without prior warning. The hybrid system may mask early warning signs.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking or see oil pressure drop, stop driving immediately.

problems.recommendation: The SDV6 Hybrid shares the 306DT diesel engine with the standard SDV6, and therefore inherits the same crankshaft‑bearing vulnerability. The hybrid system's additional weight (the vehicle weighs 2,429 kg) may place extra stress on the engine bearings. JLR issued a 10‑year/120,000‑mile extended warranty on crankshaft failure for certain models. A strict 5,000‑mile oil change interval is the single most important preventative measure — JLR's "long life" intervals are considered by specialists to be a significant contributor to premature failure.

problems.title: Hybrid Transmission Clutch Plate Wear (NHTSA Recall)
problems.symptoms: Loss of drive, transmission shifting into Neutral unexpectedly, reduced oil pressure warning, dashboard warnings.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the vehicle unexpectedly shifts into Neutral while driving, this is a safety-critical issue.

problems.recommendation: A concern has been identified where the clutch plates within the hybrid transmission may have been manufactured with a flatness out of tolerance to the engineering specification. Reduced oil pressure can trigger the transmission to shift into Neutral gear and cause loss of drive. Unexpected loss of drive at speed increases the risk of a crash. Check your VIN against the NHTSA recall database immediately. If your vehicle displays any transmission warning, have it inspected without delay.

problems.title: Hybrid Battery Degradation / Failure
problems.symptoms: Hybrid system not working, vehicle running only on diesel, low‑voltage battery not charging, dashboard warning messages, reduced fuel economy.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — battery replacement only (if ignored, the vehicle runs as a conventional diesel only).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if hybrid system warnings appear.

problems.recommendation: The SDV6 Hybrid's lithium‑ion battery is now 8‑12 years old and may be approaching the end of its useful life. Some owners report that dealers are unable to replace the hybrid battery as it is no longer available. Specialist hybrid battery refurbishment companies may offer a solution. If the hybrid system fails, the vehicle will run on diesel only, which reduces fuel economy from 45 mpg to approximately 30‑35 mpg. Have the hybrid system diagnosed by a specialist who understands the 306DT hybrid powertrain.

problems.title: DPF Blockage (Urban Driving)
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light, DPF warning message, failed emissions test.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if DPF warning appears.

problems.recommendation: The SDV6 Hybrid is designed for long‑distance motorway driving. If used primarily for short urban trips, the DPF will clog. The hybrid system may encourage electric‑only driving at low speeds, which can exacerbate DPF issues as the diesel engine doesn't get hot enough to regenerate the filter. Regular motorway runs (30+ minutes at 60+ mph) are essential to maintain the exhaust filtration system.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Full engine replacement — rebuild rarely cost‑effective
Essential safety repair — check recall status first
Specialist refurbishment may be possible; dealer replacement may be NLA
Worth fixing unless engine is already compromised

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with documented 5,000‑mile oil changes
* JLR crankshaft extended warranty still in place (2012–2015 models)
* Hybrid battery health check completed and documented
* Transmission recall work verified as completed
avoidIf: * No evidence of regular oil changes (5,000‑mile intervals) — this is critical
* Any sign of knocking or oil pressure warning
* Hybrid system warnings on the dashboard — batteries are expensive and may be NLA
* No record of transmission recall work — clutch plate failure is safety‑critical

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid was a pioneering vehicle — the first premium diesel‑electric hybrid SUV. It combines the torque and range of the 306DT V6 diesel with a 35 kW electric motor for improved urban efficiency. However, it inherits the SDV6's crankshaft vulnerability and adds hybrid‑specific complexity — transmission clutch plate wear, battery degradation, and the potential for parts becoming unavailable. Buyers should prioritise vehicles with full service history, documented crankshaft warranty coverage, and evidence of hybrid system health. A well‑maintained SDV6 Hybrid offers exceptional real‑world fuel economy (45 mpg) and effortless torque; a neglected example can be ruinously expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport SDV6 Hybrid (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any knocking? Any smoke? (knock = stop immediately)
2. Service History — 5,000‑mile oil changes? This is non‑negotiable
3. Crankshaft Warranty — Is the 10‑year/120,000‑mile JLR extended warranty active?
4. Hybrid System — Does the hybrid system work? Any dashboard warnings?
5. Transmission — Any hesitation, clunking, or unexpected Neutral shifts?
6. Battery Health — Has the hybrid battery been tested? What's its state of health?
7. DPF History — Has the vehicle done regular longer runs? (short trips kill DPFs)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
